raw_consumer_statement:
Complaint Regarding Overcharge on American Express XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, American Express does not protect customers. 

Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding my American Express XXXX card and an unresolved overcharge from a transaction with XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

On XX/XX/year>, during my visit to XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was overcharged for a service that the resort representative had clearly stated was a complimentary replacement for a service that was already included in the package I had paid for. I would not have agreed to the service had I known it would incur an additional cost. However, despite their assurance, I was still charged {$320.00}. The resort informed me that if I did not pay the amount, I would not be able to leave the island. 

Under pressure, I paid the bill but included a written notice of my disagreement with the charge at the time of signing the bill. 

I have contacted American Express multiple times, in both XXXX and XXXX of 2024, to dispute this charge. Despite submitting all necessary documents and clearly explaining that I was coerced into paying, American Express has refused to investigate the issue. They have simply responded by citing terms of the cardmember agreement and merchant policies, disregarding the fact that I was forced into the payment under unethical circumstances and had clearly expressed my disagreement with the charge. 

I am seeking assistance from your agency to protect consumers from such unethical and potentially illegal business practices. I believe I have been wronged both by XXXX XXXX XXXX and by American Express, who have failed to uphold their responsibilities to investigate a clear case of customer exploitation. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your assistance in resolving this issue. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, ca, XXXX email : XXXX phone : XXXX
